output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? The 60-Second Fix Tenants Swear By
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights and send a legal demand letter in 60 seconds for only $19.99—no sign-up needed.
content_outline: Why Landlords Get Away With Withholding Security Deposits
What Your Landlord Can (and Cannot) Legally Deduct
State-by-State Deadlines: How Long Is Too Long?
How to Write a Security Deposit Dispute Letter That Actually Works
Getting Your Deposit Back Without a Lawyer or Subscription
key_points: Most U.S. states require landlords to return security deposits within 14–30 days and provide an itemized deduction list.
Landlords cannot legally withhold deposits for normal wear and tear, routine cleaning, or unsubstantiated damages.
A formal letter of demand is often a legal prerequisite to suing in small claims court and signals serious intent.
Deposits-Back.com lets tenants generate a state-specific, legally-backed demand letter in 60 seconds for a flat $19.99 fee with no sign-up or subscription.
output.cta: Stop letting your landlord keep what belongs to you. Generate your state-specific security deposit demand letter at Deposits-Back.com in 60 seconds—just $19.99, no sign-up or subscription required.
output.target_audience: Frustrated U.S. renters whose landlords are withholding, delaying, or unfairly deducting from their security deposits and who need fast, affordable legal leverage without hiring an attorney.

short_form_scripts.hook: Your landlord is praying you don't know this about security deposits...
short_form_scripts.body: In most states, they have 14 to 30 days to return your money. After that, they're breaking the law. And no, 'cleaning fees' and 'fresh paint' are not valid deductions. You don't need a lawyer. You need a paper trail that proves you know your rights.
short_form_scripts.cta: Click the link to send a legally backed demand letter in 60 seconds. Just $19.99. No sign-up. No subscription. Just your money back.

short_form_scripts.hook: If your landlord said 'we're keeping it for cleaning,' watch this immediately.
short_form_scripts.body: Normal wear and tear is not your financial responsibility. They can't charge for carpet you walked on, walls you lived with, or bulbs that burned out. If they try, they're hoping you won't fight back. A dispute letter changes the game instantly.
